name: Gus Arnheim
abstract: Gus Arnheim (September 4, 1897 – January 19, 1955) was an American pianist and an early popular band leader. He is noted for writing several songs with his first hit being "I Cried for You" from 1923.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Gus Arnheim (PHONETIC). I was working at the Sebastian's Hide Cotton Club with Les Haight (PHONETIC) at the time that Bing Crosby was doing his -- making his big hits... Gus Arnheim's band out to the old Coconut Grove

about_quotes.speaker: George Shearing
about_quotes.evidence: You’d hear Benny Goodman and Woody Herman, Gus Arnheim, Coon Saunders, Lunceford of course, and Basie and Andy Kirk and Ellington.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Shearing interviewed by Joe Williams, New York City, New York, March 8, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: It goes on and on. Gus Arnheim, Andy Kirk. You stop and you say, my goodness, how many of them were there?
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Gus Arnheim and oh, many different bands that played these hotels and spots around town

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Lopez
about_quotes.evidence: Gus was with us. Gus Miller, he was with us. At the Inn and down at the Ambassador.
